definition: DXY at 103-107 despite Fed cutting 100-150bp and narrowing rate differential, sustained by technology investment inflows rather than rate differentials. TIC data shows net equity inflows $40-60B/month concentrated in technology. EPFR: $300B+ into US tech-focused funds vs. $45B EM outflows over 18 months. The 'capex dollar' operates orthogonally to rate differentials, paralleling the 1997-2001 dot-com DXY appreciation (~25%) when the Fed was not systematically tighter than peers.
evidence: DXY at 103-107 despite 100-150bp Fed cuts and narrowing rate differential
TIC data: net equity inflows $40-60B/month concentrated in technology
EPFR: $300B+ into US tech funds vs. $45B EM outflows over 18 months
BEA financial account: $300-400B annual net inflows to US equities
pair_2: 'highest-confidence shared claim across both FX analyses'
open_questions: At what capex-to-revenue ratio do technology investment inflows reverse?
How rapidly could the captive flow premium (3-5% of dollar overvaluation) unwind?
Does the dot-com parallel (DXY +25% then correction) apply given different fiscal/geopolitical backdrop?
